message:
fix(cli): scan prior words for subcommand detection in shell completions

All four completion generators (Bash, Zsh, PowerShell, Elvish) previously
identified the active subcommand by checking a fixed positional index
(e.g., COMP_WORDS[1] in Bash, words[2] in Zsh). This broke completion
when global options preceded the subcommand—for example:

  mycli --verbose run --<TAB>

would look at "--verbose" as the subcommand and offer no run-specific
options.

Now each generator scans backward through prior words/elements to find
the first token that matches a known subcommand name:

- Bash: iterates idx=1..COMP_CWORD, matches against subcommand names
- Zsh: iterates idx=2..CURRENT over $words array
- PowerShell: iterates $i=1..$scanCount over CommandElements
- Elvish: iterates over $args[..-1] with if/elif chain

Additional improvements:
- When no subcommand has been identified yet, completions now include
  both subcommand names AND global options (previously only offered at
  position 1).
- Zsh: renamed internal array from current_options to current_items
  since it may contain both commands and options.
- Elvish: subcommand-specific options are now keyed off $subcommand
  variable rather than $args[0].

Four new tests verify each shell's output contains the scanning loop
and avoids the old fixed-index pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
